incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2, believing more symbols means more efficiency

params.description: Scale of 5 graph highlights to show fewer symbols.

steps.text: Scale of 2 uses more symbols to show the same amount. Efficient means fewer symbols. Look at how many symbols the scale of 5 graph needs.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Scale of 5 graph highlights, showing fewer symbols represent the same data.

steps.text: Let me show you. Both graphs show the same number of items. But scale of 5 uses fewer symbols. That's what makes it more efficient. When we have bigger numbers, scale of 5 saves space. The answer is Scale of 5.
